figures.caption: TABLE I MAIN RESULTS ON COIN-6 AT C = 5. AA AND PLASTICITY ARE IN %; BWT AND FWT ARE PERCENTAGE POINTS. RED IS THE BEST AND BLUE THE SECOND ACROSS COMPETITIVE METHODS WITHIN EACH COLUMN. Italic gray ROWS ARE REFERENCE BOUNDS (ZERO-SHOT FLOOR AND JOINT-TRAINING ORACLE), NOT RANKED.

TABLE II MAIN RESULTS ON COIN-LONG-10 (LONG-SEQUENCE STRESS TEST) AT C = 5. AA AND PLASTICITY ARE IN %; BWT AND FWT ARE PERCENTAGE POINTS. RED IS THE BEST AND BLUE THE SECOND ACROSS COMPETITIVE METHODS WITHIN EACH COLUMN. Italic gray ROWS ARE REFERENCE BOUNDS (ZERO-SHOT FLOOR AND JOINT-TRAINING ORACLE), NOT RANKED.
TABLE III COMPONENT ABLATION OF PRISM ON COIN-6 AT β = 0.1, C = 5. EACH ROW REMOVES A SINGLE MECHANISM; ∆AA IS THE DROP RELATIVE TO THE FULL MODEL. AA IS IN %; BWT IS IN PERCENTAGE POINTS.

Fig. 6. Routing-projection symbiosis on CoIN-Long-10: per-expert null-space consumption over the 10-task sequence. The activation-subspace baseline FedGPM saturates fastest (red dotted) as Proposition 2 predicts, the monolithic gradient-subspace Fed-KeepLoRA follows (red dashed), while PRISM’s four experts share the load heterogeneously and retain capacity, consistent with the load-matching role of polarized routing in Section IV-D. Task-expert routing heatmaps after router freeze are deferred to Supp B.
